title: Creating the Content Brief
content_md:
# Creating the Content Brief

- Code: bc01ae80
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=bc01ae8001b444d9acfb9f1307a06b3f
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/7596d5c786c846e7adb9b87273f4a78c?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 2m 49s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: Okay, so here is what the content brief looks like. This is what we give the copywriters so they know exactly what they're going to write
So when we give it to them the copywriter manager is going to put the URL in here
If it's a new page and most of them are new pages, then we're going to pre-fill in what we want the final live URL to be
Generally, the URL structure is not super important. Don't spend a huge amount of time on this
Usually we're going to try to minimize the number of folders keep it flash
Keep the structure flat use dashes instead of folders and we want the target keyword in the URL
Then we have the target keyword
What target keyword is the content being written for made a title made a description are going to be left blank the content writer
Will fill that in and zero GPT score will also we left blank the copywriter will fill that in now
We have a blank content brief for every single client what that lets us do is put the client GVP information in here
So we have their business name primary category address phone number and a link to the GVP
This is helpful both for the content writers and for future steps of this process
So I would highly encourage you to do this. This basically means that we have a blank content brief for every single client
I know it doesn't seem like a lot to copy paste information for each client into this copy brief into the content brief
But if it takes one or two minutes and you're producing 100 articles a month, that's two hours of time
Okay, so we just have a blank sheet for every single client where we have this information filled in there are two here
This particular client was multi location. So for a multi location client
We're just going to put every one of their GVPs information in here
So we can copy this delete it if it delete one of these if it's not multi location
But I don't want a blank content brief for every single location
I just want one for every single client in terms of which location this content is for
That will be made very clear based on the target keyword
Remember the target keyword will almost always have the city name in it. So which GVP we're writing for will be very clear
Based on that. Okay, and then the content brief itself. We're going to repeat what client again
This is prefilled for the blank ones. Then we're going to put the page type is a
neighborhood content supporting content a category content service content, whatever it happens to be
Then we have the due date. That's assigned when the copywriter is assigned to the task any specific notes
And then we're going to add the internal links that we want the content to
Have this will also be done by the copywriter manager
When this is filled out before it's given to the copywriter
Then the copywriter will go through the copywriting process and paste the finalized content in here
And they'll update made a title made a description and zero gpt score
Everything else will already be filled in for the copywriter to have the necessary information they need
